methodsAttempted.query: site:durham.ac.uk Durham University generative AI policy students academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found Durham Common Awards AI academic-misconduct guidance, the linked Common Awards PDF, DCAD generative-AI teaching resources, and research-integrity pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:durham.ac.uk Durham University artificial intelligence assessment guidance generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found DCAD learning, teaching, and assessment guidance, Global Opportunities AI guidance, and older/event-style DCAD material.

methodsAttempted.query: site:durham.ac.uk Durham University AI tools data protection Microsoft Copilot generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search did not find a public Durham IT approved-tool policy page; the public DCAD resource page linked to internal SharePoint CIS guidance.

Followed official links from the DCAD generative-AI resources page to public assessment guidance, a public student micro-course overview, and internal SharePoint sources for institutional, CIS, and research guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official Durham discovery found public, claim-bearing sources for Common Awards academic-misconduct rules, DCAD learning/teaching/assessment resources, DCAD assessment-design guidance, and Global Opportunities application guidance. The internal institutional GenAI policy, CIS GenAI policy, and research GenAI guidance resolved to Microsoft sign-in and were not accessed. The public student guide exposed only overview and lesson-title text, and the public research-integrity policy only linked to internal AI research guidance, so those were rejected for policy-claim extraction.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Misconduct: AI Policy and Guidance
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In general, however, other limited uses of generative AI to facilitate your work do not count as academic misconduct, provided that the resulting work still reflects your own engagement with your sources, your own understanding, and your own reasoning and judgments; you clearly acknowledge any use of AI that has substantially informed the content or presentation of your work; and you demonstrate appropriate caution about the limitations of the tools you use.

artifacts.title: Durham University staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Durham University uses official Durham-controlled sources only. Approved claims cover Common Awards academic-misconduct rules for generative AI in summative assessments, public DCAD source-status and assessment-design guidance, and Global Opportunities application guidance. Blocked or rejected sources include internal SharePoint institutional, CIS, and research GenAI guidance, a public student-guide overview that did not expose substantive guidance text, the public research-integrity policy that only linked to internal AI research guidance, and a duplicate Common Awards PDF.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Common Awards claims are scoped to Common Awards modules and should not be read as a university-wide Durham policy for every module or assessment.
DCAD assessment claims are guidance about teaching and assessment design, not binding student conduct rules.
Global Opportunities claims are scoped to Global Opportunities applications and personal statements.
Internal SharePoint sources, including the institutional GenAI policy, CIS GenAI policy, and research GenAI guidance, required Microsoft sign-in and were not accessed or used as evidence.
The public student guide exposed only overview and lesson-title text during this run, so it was not used for substantive policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
